Title: Discovery and characterization of small molecule inhibitors of cystathionine gamma-synthase with in planta activity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Escherichia coli; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: E. coli LE392 MetB knockout; Arabidopsis thaliana accession NA; BY-2 tobacco cell line
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Methionine biosynthesis / aspartate-family amino acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Transsulfuration branch from O-phospho-homoserine to cystathionine
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent aminotransferase class / cystathionine gamma-synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses O-phospho-homoserine with cysteine to form cystathionine, releasing phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid required for plant viability and growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CgS activity promotes methionine biosynthesis; small-molecule inhibition of AtCgS suppresses methionine-pathway-dependent bacterial growth, inhibits tobacco BY-2 cell viability, and reduces Arabidopsis seedling root growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous complementation of MetB knockout E. coli; rescue by exogenous methionine; inhibition by known inhibitor PAG; recombinant protein binding by MST; in vitro enzyme inhibition assay; BY-2 viability assay; Arabidopsis seedling metabolically linked growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed from plasmid in E. coli assay; endogenous plant expression not characterized in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast (transit peptide mentioned; assay used transit-peptide-lacking protein)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible bacterial expression from PQE-30 and pET-11 systems
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous methionine supplementation; treatment with CGSI compounds; agar plate seedling assay conditions; dark-grown BY-2 culture
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential off-target activity toward structurally related human cystathionine gamma-lyase; field efficacy, spray performance, stability, environmental effects, and selectivity remain unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising herbicide target; could be paired with engineering or directed evolution of inhibitor-insensitive CgS alleles for crop resistance; EMS or fast-neutron mutant screens suggested for tolerant crops
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study used Arabidopsis CgS for biochemical/functional validation but docking templates came from Nicotiana tabacum CGS crystal structures; active site requires PLP and key residues Lys261 and Tyr163 are noted; small molecules CGSI-2 and CGSI-4 showed strongest in planta promise
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCgS complemented methionine-auxotrophic MetB knockout E. coli, confirming functional replacement in methionine biosynthesis. 2. Known inhibitor PAG and newly discovered CGSI compounds specifically inhibited growth only when methionine was not supplied, linking activity to the methionine pathway. 3. Recombinant CgS directly bound several CGSI molecules and its enzymatic activity was inhibited, with CGSI-2 and CGSI-3 showing strongest enzymatic inhibition. 4. CgS-targeting compounds reduced BY-2 tobacco cell viability and inhibited Arabidopsis seedling root growth, supporting in planta activity.
